list.title: Parallel Rollout Approximation for Pixel-Space Autoregressive Image Generation

extra.desc: Parallel Rollout Approximation (PRA) addresses limitations in pixel-space autoregressive image generation by using low-dimensional intermediate states and parallel training to improve quality and efficiency.

list.title: Cluster, Route, Escalate: Cascaded Framework for Cost-Aware LLM Serving

extra.desc: A cascaded approach for deploying large language models that balances accuracy and cost by routing queries to appropriate models based on clustering and quality estimation.

list.title: MultiHashFormer: Hash-based Generative Language Models

extra.desc: MultiHashFormer enables hash-based autoregression in language models by representing tokens as hash signatures processed through a Hash Encoder and Hash Decoder within a Transformer framework.

list.title: The Tatoxa System for Text Detoxification in Low-Resource Languages: The Case of Tatar

extra.desc: Tatoxa is a state-of-the-art text detoxification system for the Tatar language that demonstrates superior performance over existing LLMs and highlights the challenges of cross-lingual transfer in low-resource settings.

list.title: Thinking While Speaking: Inference-Time Knowledge Transfer for Responsive and Intelligent Conversational Voice Agents

extra.desc: Conversational infill enables small real-time models to maintain responsiveness while integrating delayed reasoning outputs, bridging the gap between latency and capability in voice agents.

list.title: GBC: Gradient-Based Connections for Optimizing Multi-Agent Systems

extra.desc: Gradient-Based Connections enables fine-grained attribution and optimization in multi-agent systems by modeling agent interactions as a computational graph and using gradient-based weights to identify error sources at the token level.

list.title: Object-Centric Residual RL for Zero-Shot Sim-to-Real VLA Enhancement

extra.desc: An object-centric residual reinforcement learning framework improves real-world vision-language-action model robustness through simulation-trained corrective policies that transfer zero-shot despite sim-to-real challenges.

list.title: Learning to Fold: prizewinning solution at LeHome Challenge 2026 (1st place online, 2nd offline)

extra.desc: A vision-language-action policy improved with reinforcement learning uses shared network predictions for success estimation and advantage calculation in bimanual garment folding, employing established RL techniques with novel optimization and deployment strategies.

list.title: Boundary-Aware Context Grounding for A Low-Channel EEG Agent

extra.desc: NeuraDock Agent combines a deterministic EEG processing engine with a language model interface to ensure accurate, hardware-aware analysis while maintaining local data security.

list.title: Translation as a Bridging Action: Transferring Manipulation Skills from Humans to Robots

extra.desc: Human manipulation skills are transferred to robots more effectively by using a bridging action representation based on relative wrist translation in the initial head-camera frame, combined with a vision-language-action model that handles embodiment differences through interleaved action tokens and attention masking.

list.title: ProMSA:Progressive Multimodal Search Agents for Knowledge-Based Visual Question Answering

extra.desc: A progressive multimodal search agent for knowledge-based visual question answering that adaptively selects search strategies and optimizes through sequence-level reinforcement learning.

list.title: Ko-WideSearch: A Korean Breadth-Search Benchmark for Exhaustive Set Enumeration by Web Agents

extra.desc: A Korean web-agent benchmark evaluates breadth of search capabilities by requiring complete enumeration of entity memberships with attribute tables, revealing consistent failures in row recovery despite accurate set identification.

list.title: NormGuard: Reward-Preserving Norm Constraints in Flow-Matching Reinforcement Learning

extra.desc: Reinforcement learning post-training degrades perceptual quality in flow-based generators through velocity norm inflation, which requires training-time intervention rather than inference-time corrections to maintain both reward alignment and image quality.

list.title: SingGuard: A Policy-Adaptive Multimodal LLM Guardrail with Dynamic Reasoning

extra.desc: SingGuard is a policy-adaptive multimodal guardrail system that evaluates safety in real-time conversations by dynamically applying natural-language rules through fast-to-slow reasoning modes.

list.title: Qwen-Image-2.0-RL Technical Report

extra.desc: A reinforcement learning and on-policy distillation approach enhances the visual quality and instruction-following capabilities of a diffusion model for image generation and editing tasks.

list.title: Formalizing Latent Thoughts: Four Axioms of Thought Representation in LLMs

extra.desc: An axiomatic evaluation framework reveals systematic failures in latent thought representations of LLMs across multiple reasoning tasks, demonstrating that current representations fail to satisfy fundamental functional axioms consistently across different model architectures.

list.title: Towards Automating Scientific Review with Google's Paper Assistant Tool

extra.desc: AI-assisted scientific review systems like PAT use advanced inference scaling to identify mathematical errors and improve research quality while maintaining human oversight.

list.title: SimFoundry: Modular and Automated Scene Generation for Policy Learning and Evaluation

extra.desc: SimFoundry enables zero-shot real-world robot policy training through automated simulation construction and diverse scene variations that improve generalization and performance prediction.

list.title: PhysisForcing: Physics Reinforced World Simulator for Robotic Manipulation

extra.desc: PhysisForcing enhances embodied video generation by enforcing physical consistency through pixel-level trajectory alignment and semantic-level relational alignment losses in a DiT-based framework.

list.title: ABACUS: Adapting Unified Foundation Model for Bridging Image Count Understanding and Generation

extra.desc: ABACUS is a unified vision-language model that performs object counting and related tasks through innovative spatial grounding, boundary-aware counting policies, and self-critical learning strategies.

list.title: Neglected Free Lunch from Post-training: Progress Advantage for LLM Agents

extra.desc: Reinforcement learning post-training enables effective step-level scoring for language models without requiring dedicated reward model training by deriving an implicit advantage function called progress advantage.

list.title: Information-Aware KV Cache Compression for Long Reasoning

extra.desc: InfoKV is an entropy-aware KV cache compression framework that enhances long-context reasoning in LLMs by incorporating information-theoretic signals alongside attention weights.

list.title: EO-WM: A Physically Informed World Model for Probabilistic Earth Observation Forecasting

extra.desc: EO-WM is a video diffusion transformer for multispectral Earth Observation forecasting that incorporates physically informed conditioning frameworks to better capture weather-driven uncertainties in land-surface dynamics.

list.title: LISA: Likelihood Score Alignment for Visual-condition Controllable Generation

extra.desc: Score-based generative modeling reveals that side networks contribute likelihood scores to conditional control, leading to improved training efficiency through likelihood score alignment regularization.

list.title: Running the Gauntlet: Re-evaluating the Capabilities of Agents Beyond Familiar Environments

extra.desc: A web-based benchmark evaluates agent generalization across challenging scenarios, revealing significant gaps between current agentic systems and human performance in temporal perception, graphical understanding, and 3D reasoning.

list.title: When Does Combining Language Models Help? A Co-Failure Ceiling on Routing, Voting, and Mixture-of-Agents Across 67 Frontier Models

extra.desc: Multi-model systems face fundamental accuracy limits determined by the rate at which all models fail simultaneously, regardless of their individual correlations or ensemble strategies.

list.title: CoffeeBench: Benchmarking Long-Horizon LLM Agents in Heterogeneous Multi-Agent Economies

extra.desc: CoffeeBench evaluates LLM agents in a multi-agent economic simulation where firms interact over 90 days to maximize profits, revealing differences in communication patterns and performance among various models.

list.title: Discretizing Reward Models

extra.desc: Reward models in reinforcement learning suffer from oversensitivity issues where they assign different scores to equally good responses, leading to poor policy learning, but this can be mitigated through discretization techniques that maintain discriminative ability while reducing oversensitivity.

list.title: JetSpec: Breaking the Scaling Ceiling of Speculative Decoding with Parallel Tree Drafting

extra.desc: JetSpec is a speculative decoding framework that combines efficient forward drafting with causal conditioning to improve LLM inference speed and acceptance rates across various benchmarks.

list.title: How Post-Training Shapes Biological Reasoning Models

extra.desc: Post-training stages in biological reasoning models differently affect generalization, with continued pre-training aligning models with biological language, supervised fine-tuning improving in-domain performance but reducing out-of-domain generalization, and reinforcement learning recovering out-of-domain performance when applied to well-aligned checkpoints.

list.title: Hallucination in World Models is Predictable and Preventable

extra.desc: World models exhibit hallucinations in low-data regions of state-action space, which can be detected and mitigated using data-centric signals and coverage-aware sampling techniques.

list.title: The Verification Horizon: No Silver Bullet for Coding Agent Rewards

extra.desc: Verification challenges in AI agents arise from the difficulty of aligning proxy signals with human intent, requiring adaptive verification systems that evolve alongside generative capabilities.

list.title: Why Multi-Step Tool-Use Reinforcement Learning Collapses and How Supervisory Signals Fix It

extra.desc: Research investigates how different supervisory signals and training strategies improve the stability and performance of large language models in tool-use tasks, addressing issues like catastrophic collapse and format sensitivity through interleaved supervised fine-tuning and reinforcement learning.

list.title: OpenBioRQ: Unsolved Biomedical Research Questions for Agents

list.title: In-Context World Modeling for Robotic Control

list.title: Confidence-Aware Tool Orchestration for Robust Video Understanding

list.title: ViQ: Text-Aligned Visual Quantized Representations at Any Resolution

list.title: OPID: On-Policy Skill Distillation for Agentic Reinforcement Learning

list.title: Fast LeWorldModel

list.title: COrigami: An AI Pipeline for Co-Designing Flat-Foldable Visually Recognisable Origami
